comment: Bullet jump (cf. Warframe): from a CROUCH or SLIDE, a directional burst that launches the player forward (along aim/look = the `forward` space) AND up. Triggered by the dedicated `bullet_jump` ability input (buffered) so it never collides with the base jump verb's slide-jump / crouch-jump (which fire on `jump`). Pure DATA: composed onto base.kit.json via kit overlay; uses ONLY existing Condition/Effect primitives (input_buffered, timer, all; add_velocity(forward), set_velocity_y, set_collider_height, set_timer). A cooldown timer (bullet_jump_cd) is the guard, expressed entirely as data. NO ENGINE CODE CHANGE. See docs/decisions/movement-substrate.md §2 (composition), Slice 4.

SLIDE.comment: Bullet jump out of a slide. Priority 95 — above the slide-jump (90) and all slide exits — so the ability fires the instant it is buffered and the cooldown is ready. Distinct input from `jump`, so no ambiguity. Stands up (with headroom), bursts forward+up, arms the cooldown.

CROUCH.comment: Bullet jump out of a crouch. Same band (95) as the slide variant; CROUCH and SLIDE are different states, so there is no collision. Stands up, bursts forward+up, arms the cooldown.
